'''Sylvain Houles''' ({{IPA|fr|silvɛ̃ ul}}; born 3 August 1981) is a French professional [[rugby league]] coach who is the head coach of [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]] and a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer.

==Playing career==
As a player, Houles was a [[France national rugby league team|French]] international who [[2001 French rugby league tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|toured New Zealand and Papua New Guinea in 2001]] and appeared in the [[Super League]] for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He also played in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Toulouse Olympique]]. His position of choice was initially on the {{rlp|wg}} or in the {{rlp|ce}}, but in his later career he played as a {{rlp|lf}}.

==Coaching career==
Houles combined playing with an assistant coach role in his later career. He was appointed assistant coach at [[Toulouse Olympique]] in May 2013, working alongside [[Gilles Dumas]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=7 June 2013 |title=Innes et Houlès avec Dumas |trans-title=Innes and Houlès with Dumas |url=https://www.ladepeche.fr/article/2013/06/07/1644240-innes-et-houles-avec-dumas.html |access-date=18 October 2025 |website=[[La Dépêche du Midi|La Dépêche]] |language=fr}}</ref> He was named head coach in December 2013.<ref>{{Cite web |date=10 May 2014 |title=Houlès : le XIII en exclusivité |trans-title=Houlès: Rugby league exclusive |url=https://www.ladepeche.fr/article/2014/05/10/1878423-houles-le-xiii-en-exclusivite.html |access-date=18 October 2025 |website=[[La Dépêche du Midi|La Dépêche]] |language=fr}}</ref>

On 10 October 2021, Houles coached Toulouse to victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[Million Pound Game]] which saw the club promoted to the [[Super League]] for the first time in their history.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12430948/million-pound-game-toulouse-promoted-to-super-league-after-beating-featherstone#:~:text=Toulouse%20beat%20part%2Dtimers%20Featherstone,preferred%20French%20team%20in%202003/|title=Toulouse promoted to Super League after beating Featherstone|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

Toulouse Olympique would go on to be relegated the following season from the Super League after finishing bottom of the table. Houles would later comment saying that the relegation had done "untold damage" to the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toulouse-suffering-untold-damage-due-to-relegation-from-super-league/|title=Toulouse suffering "untold damage" due to relegation from Super League|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|date=21 October 2022 }}</ref>

In the [[2025 RFL Championship]] season, Houles guided Toulouse to a 2nd-placed finish and victory in the grand final against [[York RLFC|York]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/25522209.sylvain-houles-proud-toulouse-beat-york-knights-grand-final/|title=Sylvain Houles 'proud' as Toulouse beat York Knights in Grand Final|website=www.yorkpress.co.uk}}</ref>
